payload.text:
Role: You are a Senior Full-Stack Developer and Software Architect specializing in React, Supabase, and Progressive Web Apps (PWAs).

Task: Build a comprehensive technical foundation and implementation plan for the "AKTIVATE Attendance App," an internal staff tracking system for an advertising agency in Burundi.

1. Project Context & Tech Stack

Goal: Precise check-in/out tracking via QR codes to manage lateness and absenteeism.

Frontend: React (Vite) with PWA capabilities (Service Workers, manifest.json).

Backend/Database: Supabase (Auth, PostgreSQL, Edge Functions, Real-time Subscriptions).

Key Constraints: Single office location, mobile-first for staff, desktop dashboard for admins, 08:30 AM late threshold, manual check-out required (no auto-checkout).

2. Database Schema (Supabase SQL)

Please use this schema as the source of truth for all data interactions:

Enums: user_role (employee, manager, hr), check_method (qr_scan, manual), request_status (pending, approved, rejected), notif_type.

Tables:

users: id, email, full_name, role, department, is_active.

attendance_records: user_id, check_in_time, check_out_time, check_in_method, location_gps, is_late (computed), manual_reason, status.

qr_codes: qr_data (encrypted), is_active, valid_from, valid_until.

notifications: user_id, title, message, is_read.

3. Core Logic & Functional Requirements

QR Logic: Generate a unique encrypted string every 7 days. On scan, validate that the QR is active and the timestamp is within the valid_until range.

The 8:30 Rule: Any check_in_time > 08:30 AM must automatically flag is_late = true and trigger an in-app notification to the employee.

GPS Capture: Request permission to capture coordinates only at the moment of scan/request. Compare against a fixed office coordinate (optional flagging if > 500m).

Manual Fallback: If QR fails, staff submit a request with a reason (min 20 chars). This requires Manager approval via the dashboard before it appears in logs.

Security: One session per user. Prevent double-scanning within 30 seconds.

4. User Interface Requirements

Employee (Mobile PWA): Minimalist. Large "Check In/Out" buttons. A 30-day history calendar color-coded: Green (On-time), Yellow (Late), Red (Absent), Blue (Manual).

Admin/HR (Web Dashboard): Real-time roster using Supabase realtime. Data tables with filtering by department/date. Export functionality to CSV/XLSX for payroll.

5. Specific Deliverables Requested

Please provide the following in your response:

Project Structure: A file directory layout for the React frontend.

Supabase Setup: The SQL DDL commands to initialize the tables, enums, and Row Level Security (RLS) policies.

QR Service: A TypeScript utility function for generating and validating the weekly encrypted QR strings.

Core Component: The React logic for the QRScanner component using html5-qrcode or a similar library.

Hooks: A custom useAttendance hook to handle check-in/out logic and GPS capture.

PWA Config: The manifest.json and service worker strategy for offline shell loading.

6. Tone and Style

Provide clean, modular, and documented code.

Prioritize security (RLS policies) to ensure employees only see their own data while HR sees everything.

Focus on the "Burundi Office" context (reliable office internet, but mobile-first usage).

**Developing the Foundation**

I've been solidifying the React (TypeScript) architecture for the "AKTIVATE Attendance App."  Tailwind CSS is integrated and I'm exploring Supabase as the core. Gemini API will be considered for smart features, after the core foundation is robust.


**Constructing the Architecture**

I'm now detailing the project structure and key components for the "AKTIVATE Attendance App."  I've sketched out the React frontend (React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind) interacting with the Supabase backend (Auth, DB, Realtime). PWA features are outlined with manifest and service worker configurations. The QR system will use encrypted strings with a 7-day expiry and the business logic considers time, location and roles. Specifically, the component split will be: metadata, index.html, index.tsx, App.tsx, types.ts, supabase.ts, constants.ts, services/qrService.ts, hooks/useAttendance.ts, components/QRScanner.tsx, components/Dashboard.tsx


**Detailing the Employee View**

I'm now focusing on the "AKTIVATE Attendance App" mobile user experience. I'm building `EmployeeView.tsx` with a color-coded history calendar using `HistoryCalendar.tsx`. I've outlined the core SQL DDL schema for clarity, documenting tables like `users`, `attendance_records`, `qr_codes`, and `notifications`. RLS policies are crucial for data security; employees see their data only. I'm exploring symmetric key encryption for QR code security, demonstrating with `SubtleCrypto` or `btoa/atob` with a salt. I'm leaning toward `react-qr-reader` for QR scanning. The mobile view will handle geolocation, Supabase interactions, and the 8:30 AM logic. Tailwind will be employed for the AKTIVATE brand aesthetic. The UI will have a mobile-first approach.


**Adding AI-Driven Insights**

My current focus is on adding an AI-powered insights section to the HR dashboard. I'll leverage Gemini API to analyze attendance data and provide summaries of manual check-in reasons. I'm also refining the component structure, including `Layout.tsx`, `ManualRequestForm.tsx`, `AdminDashboard.tsx`, and `AttendanceHistory.tsx`, to accommodate this new feature. The component file list is growing rapidly!


**Refining the Logic**

I'm presently building the "AKTIVATE Attendance App" core logic. I'm focusing on the 8:30 AM threshold, geolocation checks, and manual request approval workflow for attendance management. I've defined the employee and admin views, including check-in/out functionality, attendance history, stats, and request handling. My thought process is now on how to include robust error handling and feedback mechanisms for users as they interact with each of these core functions.
